rules.title: Default branch is protected
rules.lesson: green CI means nothing if anyone can force-push to the default branch; protection is a forge fact, so ask the forge, not a committed description of it
rules.rationale: Green CI means nothing if anyone can force-push or commit directly to the default branch; protection is a forge fact, so the rule asks the forge rather than trusting a committed description of it.
rules.fix: Add a ruleset/protected-branch rule on the default branch disallowing force-push and requiring PR flow: gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/rulesets --input <ruleset.json>.

rules.title: PR review is required
rules.lesson: review is the team’s knowledge-distribution mechanism and the lightweight gate DORA’s evidence supports; the requirement must live in forge settings, not a contributing doc
rules.rationale: Review is the team’s knowledge-distribution mechanism and the lightweight gate DORA’s evidence supports (in contrast to external heavyweight approval); the requirement must live in forge settings, not in a contributing doc.
rules.fix: Set required approving reviews ≥ 1 in the branch ruleset/protection settings.

rules.title: Required status checks gate the default branch
rules.lesson: every change built and tested, failing pipeline blocks merge; verify the enforcement mechanism (required checks on the integration branch, strict/up-to-date). Escalates to blocker at critical (deterministic forge read)
rules.rationale: Every change built and tested, failing pipeline blocks merge. The check verifies the enforcement mechanism both forges document — required checks on the integration branch, in strict/up-to-date mode so a stale branch can’t merge green. Deterministic, so the critical-tier blocker escalation is lawful.
rules.fix: In the ruleset, require the pipeline’s status checks and enable "require branches to be up to date before merging".

rules.title: Declared branching model matches observed branches
rules.lesson: the descriptor declares a model; the remote either carries that shape or the declaration is fiction. Flags the mismatch, not the model — no source condemns GitFlow
rules.rationale: The descriptor declares a model (trunk / dev-staging-main / gitflow); the remote either carries that shape or the declaration is fiction. The rule flags the mismatch, not the model — no primary source condemns GitFlow-style models, and none is enforced here.
rules.fix: Align the remote with the declaration (create/prune tier branches) or correct branching_model in the descriptor.

rules.title: No stale unmerged long-lived branches
rules.lesson: stale unmerged branches are batch-size inflation; flags staleness, never the model. A justified long-lived branch is a dated risk-acceptance
rules.rationale: Stale unmerged branches are batch-size inflation: they concentrate merge risk and quietly become the release train nobody rides. The rule flags staleness, never the branch model itself; a justified long-lived branch is a dated risk-acceptance.
rules.fix: Merge, rebase, or delete the named branches; for a deliberately long-lived branch, pipeline jdg new --kind risk-acceptance --subject BR-05 --reason "..." --review-by <date>.

rules.title: Release points are marked
rules.lesson: a marked production version is what a hotfix branches from and what "redeploy previous" points at; without tags/releases there is no shared name for "what’s live". Young repos SKIP (grace)
rules.rationale: A marked production version is what a hotfix branches from and what "redeploy previous" points at; without tags or releases there is no shared name for "what’s live". Repos younger than the grace window return not-evaluable so a trunk shop that hasn’t cut its first release is never wallpapered.
rules.fix: Tag the current production ref (git tag v0.1.0 <sha> && git push --tags) or cut a forge release; wire a release workflow if releases are pipeline-made.
